message.manifestation.hex_reliquary.rena: Stored in slot %s. Rename the held focus in an anvil to set a custom label.
Pattern constructors for list, grid, and radial manifestations.
hexcasting.category.manifestation_intent: Interactive intent primitives for manifested spells.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intro: I've long suspected a link between media and the mind, but only recently have I begun to understand its depths. Constraining the media into patterns with intent makes it manifest, forming structures that can be perceived.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intro.2: More curious still, these constructs seem capable of accepting intent.$(br2)By imposing order upon them, I can compel them to carry thought forward, translating decision into action.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.menus.crea: Takes a list of intentions, a menu title and manifests a list menu.
Takes a list of intentions, a menu title, and a number of columns to manifest a grid menu. Columns are limited to 1-10.
Takes a list of intentions and a menu title to manifest a radial button menu. Radial menus require button intents only; any non-button intent causes a mishap.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.menus.open: Opens the caster's spell drawing screen as if they had right-clicked with a staff. Requires a real player caster and does not work from splinters or no-caster environments.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.menus.clea: Clears the caster's persisted staff stack (the right-click staff stack). Requires a real player caster and does not work from splinters or no-caster environments.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.menus.exit: Exits a hex if triggered by right-click on an interactable block, and triggers that interaction.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.menus.set_: Configures post-cast sound for a Hexical charmed item.$(br2)Top of stack is sound id.$(br)Use string/text sound id (namespace:path) for sound, null to mute.
Optionally place an item entity under the sound argument to target that dropped charmed item.$(br2)Without an item entity, this operator targets a charmed item in casting hand, then offhand.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.charm_audi: Not every charm should sing the same note when it casts. I can bind a custom cast resonance to a charmed item, or quiet it completely when subtlety matters.
Configures post-cast sound for a Hexical charmed item.$(br2)Top of stack is string, text, or null.$(br)Use string/text sound id (namespace:path) to force that sound, or null to mute.
Optionally place an item entity under the sound argument to target that dropped charmed item.$(br2)Without an item entity, this operator targets a charmed item in casting hand, then offhand.
This setting is stored on the item itself and is used for both menu-triggered and right-click charm casts.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents_in: An Intent is an imposition of will on the media, shaping it into a vessel awaiting a spark of interaction to awaken it.

By themselves, intents are inert. A button waits for a press, an input for text, a slider for a nudge, and a dropdown for a selection.$(br2)But once roused, they execute their patterns with the fervor of a cast hex.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.in: Creates a button from a list of patterns and a label. When clicked, the button casts the patterns.
Creates an input field from a label. Entered text is pushed to the stack of an executed action, like from a button.
Creates a numeric input from a default value and label. The entered number is pushed to the stack of an executed action.
Creates a slider from min, max, current values, and a label. The chosen value is pushed to the stack of an executed action.
Creates a checkbox from a default checked state and label. The resulting boolean is pushed to the stack of an executed action.

Creates a selectable list from options, a max visible row count, and a label. Optionally accepts a boolean to enable multi-select. The selected option or options are pushed to the stack of an executed action.
Creates a non-interactive section from a label, acting as a sub heading.
Creates a dropdown from a list of options and a label. The selected option is pushed to the stack of an executed action.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.pr: A shaped will to be present at a distant place, bound to a position and facing.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.li: $(br2)Manifests a Relay linked from a destination vector to a target vector. Manifests on the destination in direction caster is looking. Optionally takes a redstone signal strength and emits when activated.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.un: Clears an existing link from a Manifest Relay block.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.op: $(br2)A threshold is a boundary where intent manifests. To cross it is to make the caster's will real.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.de: Unravels manifestation blocks in a radius around a vector. Only blocks your will can edit are affected.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.splinters_: In trying to recreate Greater Sentinel, I first hypothosised that strong intent was the key. Instead I split away unstable slivers of my own will: brief-minded splinters that wait, cast once, and perish.
A splinter casts as me and draws on my media, yet its ambit is only sixteen blocks from its own position.$(br2)If its cast mishaps, the splinter simply collapses rather than rebounding upon me.
Schedules a new splinter that will execute a stored payload after a delay. Player-cast splinters must begin within my own ambit.

Inputs:$(br)position: world-space vector to summon at.$(br)delay ticks: non-negative delay before execution.$(br)payload: non-empty list of iotas to store and execute.$(br2)Cost: 5 dust times (active splinters + 1).
Cast only by a splinter. Creates a successor splinter using the current splinter's stored payload and ravenmind.

Inputs:$(br)position: world-space vector for the successor.$(br)delay ticks: non-negative delay before the successor executes.$(br2)Cost: replacing at the same position is free; moving to a new position costs 0.5 dust.
Cast only by a splinter. Returns the origin vector of the currently casting splinter. Useful for self-renewal, local offsets, and checking whether a successor remained in place.
Dismisses all active splinters owned by the caster.
$(br2)Creates a smooth, stateful trail channel keyed by id. Each cast appends one new point at position; the prior point is remembered automatically.

Inputs:$(br)position: world-space vector for the new point.$(br)Segments longer than 10 blocks do not connect.$(br)color from: RGB vector (0..1) at the start of the color cycle.$(br)color to: RGB vector (0..1) at the end of the color cycle.$(br)ticks: positive transition duration for color ping-pong.$(br)id: trail identifier.$(br)type?: optional particle style (default 0).$(br2)

Types:$(br)0 dust ribbon$(br)1 sparks$(br)2 trail$(br)3 pulse beads$(br)4 end rod$(br)5 witch plume$(br)6 enchant flecks$(br)7 soul flame beadss$(br)8 solid beams$(br)9 ring lattices$(br)10 lightning arcs$(br)11 hex conjure ribbons$(br)12 hex conjure sparkss$(br)13 hex conjure trails$(br)14 dual-helix.
Color vectors tint types 0-3 and 8-14; types 4-7 use native particle colors.

Renders particles at many explicit points in one cast. This operator is independent from Hex Trail and does not keep state between casts.$(br2)Inputs:$(br)points: list where each entry is either a vector or [vector, color vector].

count: particles per point (1..4).$(br2)Limits: at most 256 points and 1024 total particles per cast. Vectors must still be in ambit.$(br)Use list chunking across casts to render very large datasets safely.
Creates large particle effects from an Equation Particle iota without pushing huge point lists through the stack each cast.

Inputs:$(br)target: vector where the cloud is created, $(br)OR$(br)anchor: entity center to follow,$(br)OR$(br)anchor + offset: entity center plus offset vector. $(br) equation: Equation Particle iota that defines point layout and color behavior.
$(br)id: numeric cloud identifier.$(br2)Using the same id updates/replaces the previous cloud channel.
$(br)Projects a solid spell circle into the world displaying a pattern for a timed duration before disintegrating into arcane particles.

$(br)Inputs:$(br)patterns: non-empty list of patterns to embed around the ring (max 48).$(br)origin: vector for circle center.$(br)facing: vector used as the circle plane normal.$(br2)ticks: visible lifetime in ticks (1..1200).$(br2)size?: optional number 1..6 (default 3).
hexcasting.page.manifestation.equation_s: The Equation Synthesizer writes an Equation Particle iota directly into a held focus. Use it when you want large procedural particle structures without hand-authoring huge point lists.

Insert a focus, open the synthesizer UI, and define x/y/z expressions with ranges and point count. Configure color mode (fixed, gradient, or expression), then write the equation to store the Equation Particle iota.
Use that stored iota with Equation Hex Cloud to render the effect in-world.$(br2)Reusing the same cloud id updates the existing channel for smoother continuous animation during loops and quines.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.hex_box.ov: The Hex Reliquary stores prepared foci in labeled slots so you can swap casting payloads quickly during rituals.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.hex_box.us: Use the reliquary to store and recall focus iotas by slot. Rename the held focus in an anvil before storing it to set a custom slot label.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.mind_vault: The Mind Vault stores villager minds as reusable profiles. The first villager sets the vault profile; later stores must match that profession and level. Stored minds rotate through cooldown after use.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.st: Stores a villager mind into a Mind Vault. Inputs are villager entity and Mind Vault position. The first villager profile locks the vault profile; only matching profession and level can be stored afterward.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.contained_: A contained splinter anchors delayed casting to a Splinter Caster, letting circles hold intent until conditions are right.
Place a focus iota in the Splinter Caster, then provide circle flow to manifest and maintain the anchored splinter.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.menu_ope: Manifestation backlash: this menu is being reopened too quickly.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.radial_m: Radial manifestation requires every entry to be an intent button.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.intent_r: Manifest Relay cannot manifest on that face; the space is blocked or unsupported.
Manifest Relay signal strength must be between 0 and 15.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.portal_n: Manifest Threshold cannot manifest. The space is occupied.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.permanen: Permanent Threshold requires a complete 4x5 deepslate frame ring at both source and destination.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.mind_vau: Mind Vault cannot hold more villagers.
Mind Vault only accepts villagers matching its locked profile.
Mind Vault has no villager available; all stored minds are cooling.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.requires: This pattern requires a staff in hand.
